conditions: The sandy bottom is clearly visible and well-lit, and the farthest (~30 ft) pilings are visible as distinct columns with some texture, though slightly hazier than the 35 ft exceptional reference. This matches the 30 ft visibility criterion (most 30 ft pilings visible but less sharp than exceptional).

The closest 4 ft piling at right is sharp with clear texture. The mid-right (~11 ft) and back-left (~14 ft) pilings are visible but somewhat hazy; the 14 ft piling is more of a silhouette without clear texture. The far 30 ft pilings are not distinctly visible as separate structures, and the scene fades into blue before that distance. Sandy bottom is visible beneath but distance-limiting factor is water clarity, which matches the guideline for when the 14 ft piling is hazy/faded silhouette.

Closest 4 ft piling on right is very sharp with clear encrusting organisms. The mid/right and left pilings around 11–14 ft are visible but somewhat hazy. The far 30 ft pilings are not distinctly visible as separate silhouettes; background fades into uniform blue-green. This corresponds to clarity reaching solidly to the 14 ft piling but not to 30 ft.

Only the nearest right-edge piling is clearly visible with texture; mid and far pilings are either not present in frame or too obscured by haze to distinguish, and the water column is very murky with no clear structures beyond the closest piling.

The closest 4 ft piling (right edge) is sharp with clear biological texture. The 11 ft piling is visible but somewhat hazy, and the 14 ft piling is present as a soft silhouette with little detail. The far 30 ft pilings are not distinctly visible as separate structures. This matches conditions where the 14 ft piling is hazy/faded, corresponding to ~15 ft visibility.

Rightmost 4 ft piling is clear with good texture. The 11 ft piling behind it is visible but quite hazy and low-contrast, and the 14+ ft structures are only faint silhouettes. This matches the guideline where the 14 ft piling is hazy/faded, indicating about 15 ft visibility.
